name: Sales — AEs & SDRs
description: Quota-carrying sellers and pipeline generators who live inside their CRM and sequencing tools, battling stale contact data, low connect rates, and too many disconnected point solutions to hit number. They need accurate direct dials, verified emails, and AI-assisted workflows that reduce research time and get them in front of the right buyers faster.
company_size: Enterprise and upper mid-market (200+ employees; ZoomInfo targets companies with complex GTM motions and dedicated sales teams)
primary_pain: Tool fragmentation, stale contact data, and low connect rates that erode selling time and make it nearly impossible to hit quota consistently.

pain_points.title: Stale and inaccurate contact data kills outreach before it starts
pain_points.example: I spend half my morning trying to find a working direct dial. Half the numbers in our system are wrong, and when I finally get through, it's the wrong person. My bounce rate is through the roof and I'm worried it's tanking our domain reputation.

pain_points.title: Too many tools, too much context-switching, not enough selling time
pain_points.example: I'm toggling between LinkedIn, our CRM, a sequencing tool, an intent platform, and some enrichment thing ops set up. By the time I've stitched together enough context to write a decent email, I've burned 45 minutes on one prospect.

pain_points.title: No signal on which accounts are actually in-market right now
pain_points.example: My territory has 300 accounts. I have no idea which ones are actually looking to buy right now versus which ones I'm just cold-calling into a wall. I end up working the ones I know, not the ones that are ready.

pain_points.title: Manual research eats into quota-carrying hours
pain_points.example: Before any discovery call I'm spending 20-30 minutes pulling together org chart info, recent news, tech stack — stuff that should just be there. That's time I'm not prospecting or closing.

pain_points.title: Outreach sequences go cold because emails bounce and calls don't connect
pain_points.example: I'll build out a whole sequence and then watch the open rates crater because a third of the emails bounced. And the phone numbers — I'm hitting voicemails for people who left the company two years ago. It's demoralizing and it's killing my pipeline.

pain_points.title: Buying committee blind spots cause late-stage deal surprises
pain_points.example: We got to legal and suddenly there's a CFO and a procurement lead I'd never talked to. Nobody told me they were involved. I had no idea who the real stakeholders were until it was almost too late.

goals: Hit or exceed quota consistently by spending more time selling and less time on manual research and data cleanup
Reach the right decision-makers faster with verified direct dials and emails that actually land in the inbox

Walk into every conversation with the context needed to make it compelling — knowing what's happening in the account, what the prospect cares about, and why now — because reaching the right person means nothing if the conversation doesn't land
Prioritize the accounts most likely to convert right now using real buying signals, not gut instinct
Reduce the number of tools in the daily workflow so context stays in one place and nothing falls through the cracks
Build pipeline predictably by identifying in-market accounts before competitors do
emotional_state: Skeptical and quota-obsessed — they've been burned by tools that promised pipeline and delivered dashboards, so they evaluate everything through a single lens: 'will this help me hit or exceed number this quarter?' Hitting quota isn't just about commission — it's job security and the clearest path to promotion, so anything that makes that outcome more achievable or more predictable is an immediate win. They don't have time for tools that ops wants them to log into; they have time for things that move deals.
decision_drivers: Reduce time spent on non-selling activities so more hours go toward pipeline and closing
Eliminate the embarrassment and wasted effort of calling wrong numbers or emailing bounced addresses
Get prioritization signals that tell them where to focus without requiring manual analysis
Consolidate tools to reduce cognitive load and context-switching during the selling day
Demonstrate personal productivity gains that make their number more attainable and their manager more confident in them
Avoid the career risk of a missed quarter caused by bad data, poor territory coverage, or lack of context
messaging_angle: Write like a seasoned sales peer who has lived the pain of bad data and tool sprawl — lead with the concrete, time-wasting problem, then show the specific mechanism that fixes it; never pitch features, always translate to quota impact.
proof_point_types: Named customer outcomes with specific metrics (e.g., Seismic: 54% productivity gain, 11.5 hrs/week saved)
Data scale and accuracy stats that directly address connect rate anxiety (e.g., 120M direct-dial phone numbers, 200M+ verified business emails)
Speed-to-pipeline proof points (e.g., Databricks: 50% faster prospect reach)
Quota attainment outcomes from peer companies (e.g., Thomson Reuters: 115% avg monthly quota attainment, 40% increase in closed-won)
G2 and peer review rankings that validate real-user trust (133 No. 1 rankings including Sales Intelligence and Data Quality)
Analyst validation that signals enterprise-grade reliability (Forrester Leader, Gartner Leader)
tone_modifiers: Direct and no-fluff — get to the point fast, this persona has no patience for preamble
Peer-level credibility — write as someone who understands what a bad connect rate does to a Monday morning, not as a marketer describing a sales problem from the outside
Outcome-first — lead with what changes for the rep, then explain how
Specific over vague — name the number, the customer, the time saved; never use 'industry-leading' without a citation
Avoid AI buzzwords — say 'AI-drafted outreach in GTM Workspace' not 'AI-powered sales tool'
experience_signals: Content written by or featuring active quota-carrying sellers, not just sales leaders or analysts
References to real daily workflow friction: CRM logging, sequence building, call blocks, discovery prep
Acknowledgment of the specific pain of bounced emails and wrong numbers — not just 'data quality' in the abstract
Familiarity with the tools this persona already uses: Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, Salesloft, LinkedIn Sales Navigator
expertise_signals: Accurate use of sales-specific terminology: direct dial, connect rate, sender reputation, buying committee, multi-threading, sequence, pipeline coverage ratio
Understanding of how data quality problems cascade — bad email → bounce → domain reputation → deliverability → pipeline
Ability to distinguish between SDR and AE use cases without conflating them
Knowledge of how intent signals translate into prioritization decisions, not just awareness that intent exists
authority_signals: Named customer proof points with specific, verifiable outcomes (Seismic, Databricks, Thomson Reuters)
Third-party validation from Forrester, Gartner, and G2 cited with specifics — not just 'award-winning'
Data scale claims grounded in documented figures: 120M direct-dial numbers, 200M+ verified business emails
CEO-level statements from earnings calls that signal product conviction and competitive confidence
Fortune 500 RFP outcome cited as independent third-party validation of data superiority
trust_signals: Honest acknowledgment of what ZoomInfo does not solve — this persona has been oversold before and will disengage at the first whiff of hype
Peer company names they recognize (Snowflake, Databricks, Seismic) used as social proof, not just logos
Compliance and data privacy credentials (SOC 2 Type II, GDPR/CCPA) — relevant when enterprise IT or legal is involved in the tool evaluation
G2 review volume and rankings as a signal that real sellers, not just analysts, validated the product
Transparent framing of how GTM Workspace deploys — 'weeks, not months' — because implementation risk is a real objection at this level
